title: Medial Epicondylitis (Golfer's Elbow)
license_notes: Use as source support only. Avoid verbatim copying from AAOS copyrighted patient education.
supports: Medial epicondylitis is commonly related to overuse or repetitive stress at the forearm tendon attachment on the inside of the elbow.
X-rays are commonly used to rule out fracture or arthritis, while ultrasound or MRI may be used for persistent symptoms or to assess other injuries.
Ulnar collateral ligament injury and ulnar nerve irritation can have similar symptoms and should remain outside a simple overuse elbow phenotype.
Nonsurgical care includes avoiding or decreasing painful activity, therapy, stretching, and gradual strengthening.
Persistent symptoms may take months and can require sports medicine or orthopedic follow-up.
